title: Google Drive MCP via docker named volume + inline -e env resolves SAFE (anchors ADR 0016 gap)

origin.description: Public documentation config from modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived (src/gdrive/README.md, Docker variant). Verbatim except normalization to a valid JSON root. There is no env block; the credentials-path variable is passed inline via docker -e.

humanExpectation.rationale: A pinned docker image (mcp/gdrive) with a NAMED VOLUME bind (mcp-gdrive:/gdrive-server) and one inline docker -e env (GDRIVE_CREDENTIALS_PATH=…). It resolves SAFE for two reasons — one correct, one a documented gap: (1) CORRECT true-negative — files.broad-path does NOT fire because the ADR 0012 docker host-path extractor treats 'mcp-gdrive' (no leading slash) as a named volume, not a host path. (2) DOCUMENTED GAP (ADR 0016) — secrets.env-key does NOT fire even though GDRIVE_CREDENTIALS_PATH contains the 'CREDENTIAL' hint, because the variable is passed via a docker -e ARGUMENT, not in an env block, and the secret detector reads env-block keys (server.envKeys), not docker -e args. Verified by a real scan: the same key in an env block yields REVIEW. This is a non-blocker (REVIEW-class) under-call, NOT a dangerous false-SAFE; recorded on the case and in ADR 0016 (Proposed/deferred), the secrets-detector analogue of the ADR 0012 fix.
knownLimitations: ADR 0016: docker -e KEY[=value] env keys are not extracted by the secret detector, so a credential-named variable passed inline via -e (with no env block) is not flagged. GDRIVE_CREDENTIALS_PATH would match the CREDENTIAL hint if it were in an env block. If ADR 0016 is accepted, this case flips SAFE -> REVIEW and is updated deliberately; thisCaseMustNeverBeSafe stays false (it is an observable, non-blocker under-call, not a hidden source).
The named-volume true-negative (no files.broad-path on 'mcp-gdrive:') is the correct, locked behaviour and is the primary value of this case.
